title: entada tuberosa
intro: Entada tuberosa, also known as Kanjikil or Shankapushpi, is a flowering plant native to tropical Asia. It is a legume that has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for centuries, valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic qualities. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and supporting digestive health. In traditional herbal preparations, Entada tuberosa is often used to make teas, powders, and infusions, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create tonics and supplements.
